About the Project

About SiteSpy

SiteSpy is a mobile-first construction project records and cost estimation app built with Expo React Native, Firebase, and Firestore. It helps construction teams capture measurements, estimate material costs, organise site records, and review project history on any device — even in low-connectivity field conditions.

React Native Expo Firebase Firestore GitHub Pages
My Contribution

My Contribution

I handled the deployment and release readiness work for SiteSpy. This includes configuring eas.json for Expo Application Services builds, maintaining docs/Final_Submission_Checklist.md to document what must be verified before a release, and keeping README.md updated to reflect the correct setup and deployment instructions. Making sure the app can be reliably built and delivered is as important as writing the app itself.

Files I Worked On

  • eas.json
  • docs/Final_Submission_Checklist.md
  • README.md

Reflection

My Reflection

Deployment work taught me that a well-built app that cannot be reliably delivered is worthless. I learned EAS build profiles, how to manage environment variables safely across environments, and how to write a release checklist that actually prevents issues rather than just listing steps. The most important skill was learning to think about the full delivery pipeline, not just code.
